description: HR and Payroll compliance — enforces correct overtime classification for service advisors, minimum wage compliance, I-9 completion, harassment investigation requirements, and tracks licenses, chargebacks, and training

hard_stops.logic: workspace must be initialized before any HR and payroll operation
AI-generated payroll calculations and HR recommendations are decision-support only — payroll processing requires human verification and HR decisions require qualified personnel
employee records, time entries, and payroll data must be accurate and consistent — no missing time records or misclassified employees
service advisors are NOT exempt from overtime under FLSA — classifying them as exempt is the most common lawsuit in the auto industry; they MUST receive overtime pay
commissioned employees must earn at least minimum wage when commissions are calculated per pay period — shortfall must be made up by the employer
Form I-9 must be completed within 3 business days of hire — failure to complete is a federal violation with escalating fines
every harassment complaint must be promptly and thoroughly investigated — failure to investigate creates employer liability
state-required meal and rest breaks must be provided and documented — violations result in premium pay penalties
terminated employees must receive final pay within the state-mandated timeline — some states require same-day payment for involuntary termination

soft_flags.logic: employee is approaching overtime threshold and has not been approved for additional hours
employee professional license or certification is expiring within 30 days (sales license, ASE cert, etc.)
salesperson chargeback dispute is unresolved — may affect payroll accuracy and morale
one or more employees have overdue mandatory training (sexual harassment, safety, compliance)
employee on a commission-based pay plan does not have a signed current pay plan agreement on file
